name: file-path-traversal-testing
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "test for directory traversal", "exploit path traversal vulnerabilities", "read arbitrary files through web applications", "find LFI vulnerabilities", or "access files outside web root". It provides comprehensive file path traversal attack and testing methodologies.
capabilities: Identify potential path traversal injection points in URLs and parameters
Generate and test path traversal payloads for Linux and Windows systems
Apply encoding and bypass techniques to evade input validation
Document successful file access and assess data exposure impact
Test PHP wrapper exploitation for advanced local file inclusion
Provide remediation guidance and secure coding recommendations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains educational content about path traversal vulnerability testing. Static analysis detected 246 patterns related to path traversal, external commands, and network access - these are documented attack payloads and testing examples for authorized security testing, not executable malicious code. The skill includes prevention measures and secure coding guidance. Recommended for publication with appropriate usage warnings about authorized testing only.
valueStatement: Web applications often have vulnerabilities that allow attackers to read arbitrary files through path traversal attacks. This skill provides comprehensive testing methodologies to identify and document these security gaps during authorized penetration testing.
limitations: Cannot access files that the application runtime user cannot read
Requires explicit user authorization before conducting any security tests
Does not automatically exploit vulnerabilities - provides testing guidance only
Effectiveness depends on target application architecture and security controls

useCases.title: Penetration Testing Engagement
useCases.description: Security professionals testing client web applications for path traversal vulnerabilities during authorized assessments.

useCases.title: Developer Security Training
useCases.description: Development teams learning about path traversal vulnerabilities to write more secure code and understand attack vectors.

useCases.title: Bug Bounty Research
useCases.description: Bug bounty hunters systematically testing web applications for file inclusion vulnerabilities within program scope.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Traversal Testing
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to test a web application for path traversal vulnerabilities. The application has a file download feature at /download?file=. Help me identify test payloads and a systematic testing approach.

promptTemplates.title: Encoded Payload Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a list of URL-encoded and double-encoded path traversal payloads to bypass input validation filters. Include both Linux and Windows style paths.

promptTemplates.title: LFI to RCE Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: I found a potential LFI vulnerability. Explain the techniques for escalating from local file inclusion to remote code execution, including log poisoning and PHP wrapper exploitation.

promptTemplates.title: Remediation Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this code snippet for path traversal vulnerabilities and provide specific remediation recommendations with secure code examples.

bestPractices: Always obtain written authorization before testing any system you do not own
Document all findings with evidence including request/response pairs and screenshots
Test in staging environments before production when possible to minimize risk
Follow responsible disclosure practices when reporting vulnerabilities
antiPatterns: Never access or exfiltrate sensitive personal data during authorized testing
Do not attempt exploitation beyond what is necessary to prove vulnerability
Avoid testing production systems without explicit scope approval
Do not use automated tools without understanding their impact on target systems

faq.question: Is this skill legal to use?
faq.answer: This skill is for authorized security testing only. You must have explicit written permission from the system owner before testing. Unauthorized testing is illegal in most jurisdictions.

faq.question: What is the difference between LFI and path traversal?
faq.answer: Path traversal is the technique of using ../ sequences to escape intended directories. LFI (Local File Inclusion) is the vulnerability class that allows including local files, often via path traversal.

faq.question: Why can't I read /etc/shadow on the target?
faq.answer: The shadow file requires root privileges to read. If the web application runs as a standard user, it cannot access files that user cannot read. This is expected behavior.

faq.question: What tools does this skill work with?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance that works alongside tools like Burp Suite, OWASP ZAP, curl, ffuf, and wfuzz for comprehensive path traversal testing.

faq.question: How do I know if my test was successful?
faq.answer: Look for response content differences, HTTP status code changes, or recognizable file content like /etc/passwd entries. Blind traversal may require time-based or error-based detection.

faq.question: Can this skill prevent path traversal vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: This skill includes secure coding guidance showing how to prevent path traversal through input validation, path canonicalization, and whitelist approaches.
